full_episode.title: Episode 1 — The Encounter
text: My name is {{user_name}}. I work as a {{user_profession}} in {{user_city}}.
Today, I went to my cousin's wedding. The hall was bright and full of music.
I saw a woman in a white dress. She was standing near the flowers.
Her name was Layla. She had a gentle smile and kind eyes.
I looked at her for a long moment. My heart felt something new.
She noticed me watching her. She smiled and walked toward me slowly.
She said: Hello, are you a friend of the groom?
The band played a slow song, and couples moved onto the dance floor.
My cousin, the groom, waved at me from across the crowded hall.
Layla's dress had small silver details near the collar.
A waiter passed by with a tray of juice and sweet pastries.
I offered her a glass of juice, and she accepted with a nod.
I answered: Yes, he is my cousin. And you?
She replied: I am the bride's colleague. We work together.
We talked for an hour. I felt happy and a little nervous.
A photographer walked around, capturing candid moments of every guest.
Before leaving, I promised myself I would ask about her again soon.

full_episode.title: Episode 2 — The Coffee Shop
text: The next morning, I thought about Layla. I wanted to see her again.
I called my friend Sami. I said: I met a wonderful woman.
Sami laughed and said: Finally! What is her name?
I said: Her name is Layla. She works with my cousin's wife.
Sami said: Invite her for coffee. Be honest and simple.
I found a small cafe near the market. It was quiet and warm.
I sent Layla a message. I asked her to meet me there.
Sami teased me about how nervous I sounded on the phone.
I told him: This feels different from anyone I have met before.
He said: Trust your feelings, but take small steps first.
I wrote down the cafe's address so I would not forget it.
Before sleeping, I checked my phone one more time for her reply.
She replied quickly: Yes, I would love that. See you tomorrow.
I felt excited all day. I could not stop smiling.
That evening, I chose my best shirt and waited for tomorrow.
I noticed my reflection in the mirror, smiling without realizing it.
The whole street seemed brighter as I walked back that night.

full_episode.title: Episode 3 — Her Number
text: At the cafe, Layla arrived early. She was wearing a blue scarf.
We sat at a small table near the window. The coffee smelled sweet.
She told me about her job and her family. I listened carefully.
I told her about my work and my dreams for the future.
She laughed at my jokes. Her laugh was soft and beautiful.
After two hours, I asked for her phone number. She agreed happily.
I saved her number and wrote a heart next to her name.
Layla ordered a sweet pastry, and I ordered black coffee.
She spoke about a school trip to the mountains years ago.
I shared a funny memory from my own childhood in {{user_city}}.
The cafe owner smiled at us and brought free dessert.
Time passed quickly, and neither of us noticed the clock.
Before she left, she said: Thank you for a wonderful afternoon.
I walked home with a big smile. My heart felt light.
That night, I sent her a message: I really enjoyed today.
A gentle breeze moved through the open window beside our table.
She glanced at her watch, surprised how quickly the visit had passed.

full_episode.title: Episode 4 — The First Message
text: Layla replied fast: Me too. I hope we can meet again soon.
We texted every day. Every message made me happier than before.
She sent me a photo of the sunset from her balcony.
I sent her a photo of the river near my house.
One evening, she wrote: I trust you. You are different.
I felt proud and grateful. I promised to always be honest.
We talked about our dreams, our fears, and our favorite music.
She asked about my favorite season, and I said autumn.
I asked about her favorite food, and she said grilled fish.
We joked about our different opinions on loud music.
She sent a short poem she had written when she was young.
I read it twice and told her it was truly beautiful.
She told me a secret: she wanted to travel to Beirut soon.
I said: That is your city, right? Tell me more about it.
She smiled through the phone screen and said: I will show you one day.
Every message felt like a small piece of a growing puzzle.
I kept her messages saved, rereading them during quiet moments alone.

text: Layla was born in Beirut. Her family still lived near the sea.

Her father was a doctor. Her mother was a teacher.
She had one brother and one sister. They were very close.
She missed the mountains and the old streets of her city.
I asked: Do you want to visit Beirut this year?
She said: Yes, and I want you to come with me.
She described the narrow streets near her old school in detail.
Her grandmother used to tell her stories every single evening.
I asked if her grandmother was still healthy and strong.
She said: Yes, and she still cooks for the whole family.
I felt eager to meet every person she loved so much.
I felt surprised and happy. I said: I would love that.
We planned the trip together. We chose a date in the summer.
She said: My family will be excited to meet you.
She described the taste of her mother's traditional dishes in detail.
I promised that one day I would taste that cooking myself.

full_episode.title: Episode 6 — The Market
text: On Friday, Layla and I walked through the old market together.
The market was full of colors: red, yellow, green, and gold.
We bought fresh fruit, bread, and a small box of chocolate.
A man was selling flowers near the corner. I bought roses for her.
Layla's eyes were bright with joy. She hugged me softly.
We sat near a fountain and shared the chocolate together.
She said: This is the best afternoon I have had in months.
An old man played gentle music on a simple wooden instrument.
Layla stopped to listen, her eyes closed for a moment.
We bought a small gift for her mother from a nearby shop.
Children ran past us, laughing and chasing a colorful ball.
The smell of grilled food filled the narrow market streets.
I held her hand and said: I want more days like this.
She looked at me and whispered: Me too.
We walked home slowly, watching the sunset over the city.
A street artist drew our names together inside a painted heart.
We kept that small drawing as a simple, cherished souvenir.

full_episode.title: Episode 7 — The Airport
text: The next week, Layla had to travel to Beirut for her job.
I drove her to the airport early in the morning.
She held her suitcase and her passport in her hands.
I said: I will miss you every single day.
She smiled and said: I will call you when I arrive.
Her flight was announced. We hugged for a long moment.
She whispered: Wait for me. I will be back soon.
Her mother hugged her tightly before she passed through the gate.
I stood beside her family, feeling like part of the group.
Her brother patted my shoulder and said: Take care of things here.
I promised to check on her family while she was away.
The airport speaker announced other flights leaving for distant cities.
I watched the plane rise into the sky. My heart felt heavy.
That night, she called me. Her voice sounded warm and close.
She said: I already miss our walks in the market.
A soft rain began just as her plane disappeared into the clouds.
I drove home slowly, letting the radio fill the silent car.

full_episode.title: Episode 8 — Back Home
text: Two weeks later, Layla returned to {{user_city}}. I met her at the airport.
She ran toward me and hugged me tightly. I felt complete.
She said: I brought you a gift from Beirut.
It was a small silver ring with a simple design.
I said: This is beautiful. Thank you for thinking of me.
She said: My mother chose it. She wants to meet you.
I felt nervous but happy. I said: I would be honored.
Her father shook my hand firmly at the arrival gate.
I noticed Layla looked more confident after her time away.
She thanked the taxi driver and carried her own suitcase inside.
Later, we shared a simple dinner with fresh bread and soup.
She placed the ring gently on the small table beside us.
We spent the evening together, talking about her trip and my work.
She said: I realized something in Beirut. I really love you.
I looked into her eyes and said: I love you too, Layla.
Her laughter filled the small kitchen while we cooked dinner together.
I realized then how empty the house had felt without her.

full_episode.title: Episode 9 — Her Family
text: A month later, Layla invited me to meet her parents.
I was nervous. I chose a nice shirt and bought sweets.
Her mother welcomed me warmly and offered me tea and cake.
Her father looked serious. He asked many questions about my job.
I answered honestly. I explained my plans for the future.
Layla's brother joined us. He was friendly and full of jokes.
Her sister smiled at me and whispered something to Layla.
Her mother asked about my mother and father with real interest.
I described my parents' small house near the old market.
Layla's father listened, nodding slowly at every answer I gave.
Dessert arrived, and everyone relaxed into easy conversation.
Before leaving, I thanked her parents for such a warm welcome.
After dinner, her father shook my hand and said: You seem honest.
Layla walked me to the door. She said: They liked you.
I felt relieved and grateful. This family already felt like home.
Her father's library was full of old books about distant countries.
I borrowed one book, promising to return it during my next visit.

full_episode.title: Episode 10 — The Father
text: A few weeks passed. Layla's father called me for a private talk.
I felt worried. I did not know what he wanted to say.
We met at his office. He offered me a chair and coffee.
He said: My daughter is happy. I can see it in her eyes.
He asked about my salary, my job, and my future plans.
I answered every question with respect and honesty.
He said: Marriage is a big responsibility. Are you ready?
He asked how I would handle difficult times in a marriage.
I said: With patience, honesty, and constant communication.
He shared a short story about his own early marriage struggles.
I listened closely, respecting every word he chose to share.
Before I left, he offered me a warm cup of tea.
I said: Yes, sir. I love Layla and I want to protect her.
He smiled for the first time and said: You have my blessing.
I left his office feeling proud, relieved, and full of hope.
Rain tapped gently against the office window during our long talk.
Leaving that meeting, I felt lighter than I had in weeks.

full_episode.title: Episode 11 — The Trip
text: Layla and I finally traveled to Beirut together in the summer.
The plane landed near the sea. The air smelled like salt and flowers.
Her family welcomed us at the airport with hugs and laughter.
We stayed in a small hotel near the old neighborhood.
Every morning, we walked to a cafe and drank sweet coffee.
In the evening, we watched the sunset from the beach.
Layla showed me her old school and her childhood street.
We visited a small bookshop near the old city square.
Layla bought a book about the history of her country.
We ate fresh fish by the sea, watching fishing boats pass.
At night, the whole city lit up with warm golden light.
Layla said this trip felt like a dream she never wanted to end.
We visited the mountains and took many photos together.
One night, under the stars, I felt completely at peace.
Layla said: This trip made everything between us stronger.
Local fishermen offered to take us out on their small wooden boat.
We watched the horizon turn orange as our little boat returned.

full_episode.title: Episode 12 — The Accident
text: After we returned home, Layla felt sick and weak.
Her mother called me: There was a small car accident.
I rushed to the hospital. My hands were shaking with fear.
A nurse said: She is stable. The doctor is checking her now.
I waited in silence, thinking of every moment we shared.
The doctor finally said: She will be fine. Just a little pain.
I entered her room. She looked tired but she smiled at me.
A stranger who saw the accident called for help immediately.
The ambulance arrived quickly, and I followed it in my car.
Layla's brother met me outside the hospital, his face pale.
We sat together in the waiting room, saying almost nothing.
Finally, a nurse called our names and led us inside.
She whispered: I was scared, but I knew you would come.
I held her hand and said: I will always be here for you.
That accident taught me how much I truly needed her.
A kind stranger in the waiting room offered us warm tea.
I thanked him quietly, grateful for such an unexpected kindness.

full_episode.title: Episode 13 — The Proposal Dinner
text: When Layla recovered, I decided it was time to propose.
I planned a special dinner at a quiet restaurant by the river.
I chose a table with candles and a view of the water.
I bought a gold ring and hid it in my pocket.
Layla arrived wearing a beautiful dress. My heart beat fast.
We ordered soup, salad, chicken, and a sweet dessert.
During dinner, I felt nervous but ready to speak the truth.
I asked the waiter to bring the ring box only after dessert.
My hands felt cold, but my mind felt completely clear.
Layla asked why I seemed so quiet during the meal.
I smiled and said: You will understand very soon.
My heart was pounding as the waiter approached our table again.
I took her hand and said: Layla, you changed my whole life.
Suddenly, the restaurant lights became soft, and music began to play.
I stood up, held the ring, and asked: Will you marry me?
Outside the restaurant, a single street lamp lit the quiet sidewalk.
Walking home afterward, neither of us could stop smiling.

full_episode.title: Episode 15 — The Papers
text: We went to the court to sign the official marriage papers.
A judge welcomed us and asked simple questions about our plans.
Layla wore a simple, elegant dress for the occasion.
Our parents stood beside us as witnesses to the marriage.
The judge said: Congratulations, you are officially engaged.
We signed the papers and exchanged a quiet, happy smile.
Outside the court, our families took many photos together.
The court building was old, with tall windows and quiet halls.
A clerk asked for our names and checked our official papers.
Layla squeezed my hand gently while we waited our turn.
My mother whispered a small prayer before we entered the room.
Outside, sunlight fell softly on the old stone steps.
Sami hugged me and said: Finally, my friend. I am proud of you.
We celebrated with a small lunch at Layla's family house.
That night, I thanked God for bringing Layla into my life.
A soft breeze moved the court's curtains as we waited quietly.
Stepping outside afterward, sunlight felt warmer than I remembered.

full_episode.title: Episode 16 — The Rumor
text: A week later, a strange rumor started at Layla's office.
Someone said Layla was secretly engaged to another man before me.
The rumor came from a colleague named Karim, who liked Layla.
I heard the rumor from Sami. My heart felt confused and hurt.
I did not want to believe it, but doubt entered my mind.
I called Layla, but my voice sounded strange and cold.
She asked: What is wrong? You sound different today.
At work, I found it hard to focus on simple tasks.
A colleague asked if something was bothering me that day.
I said nothing, hiding my worry behind a quiet smile.
Sami noticed my mood and invited me for a walk outside.
During the walk, I finally told him everything I had heard.
I said nothing about the rumor. I just said: I am tired.
That night, I could not sleep. Jealousy and worry filled my heart.
I decided to ask her directly, face to face, the next day.
A coworker offered a kind word, not knowing what troubled me.
I thanked them quietly and returned to my desk, still distracted.

full_episode.title: Episode 17 — The Office
text: The next morning, I went to Layla's office during her lunch break.
I saw Karim standing close to her desk, smiling strangely.
Jealousy grew stronger inside me. I felt my face turn red.
Layla saw me and walked over, surprised by my visit.
She asked: Is everything okay? You look upset.
I asked directly: Who is Karim, and why do people talk about you two?
Layla looked shocked. She said: Karim is only a colleague.
My hands felt tense as I opened the office door slowly.
A few colleagues looked up, surprised to see me there.
Karim's smile faded quickly once he noticed my serious face.
Layla guided me to a quiet corner away from other desks.
I took a deep breath before asking my difficult question.
She explained: He liked me once, but I always refused him.
I felt embarrassed for doubting her without any real proof.
I said: I am sorry. I should have trusted you from the start.
A quiet hum of computers filled the space between our words.
Walking out afterward, I felt both foolish and deeply relieved.

text: Layla forgave me, but she seemed quiet for the rest of the day.
Sami called me later and said: Be careful. Karim is planning something.
I asked: What do you mean? What kind of plan?
Sami said: I heard he wants to stop your wedding somehow.
I felt anger and worry mix together inside my chest.
I decided to warn Layla's father about this new danger.
Her father listened carefully and said: Stay calm and stay honest.
I could not stop thinking about Sami's serious warning that night.
I checked my phone constantly, expecting some kind of bad news.
Layla noticed my distraction and asked gently what was wrong.
I chose not to worry her yet, hoping the danger would pass.
Instead, I focused on planning small, happy details for our wedding.
He said: Truth always wins over lies and rumors, in the end.
I trusted his advice and decided not to argue with Karim directly.
Instead, I focused on Layla and our wedding plans together.
That night, the house felt unusually quiet and still around me.
I reminded myself that patience often protects what truly matters.

full_episode.title: Episode 19 — The Misunderstanding
text: Karim sent Layla an old photo with a strange, misleading message.
The message suggested that I had lied about my past to her.
Layla called me, confused and hurt by what she had seen.
She asked: Is this true? Did you hide something important from me?
I felt shocked. I said: No, that photo is old and out of context.
I explained everything calmly, without anger or blame.
Layla listened and slowly began to understand the misunderstanding.
I stayed awake most of the night, replaying every explanation.
Layla sent a short message the next morning: I believe you.
Relief filled my chest as I read her simple, honest words.
We met for coffee to talk everything through calmly.
By the end of our talk, we both felt lighter and closer.
She said: I am sorry for doubting you. Karim wants to hurt us.
We agreed to ignore his messages and stay strong together.
That night, our trust became stronger than before.
A single candle burned on the table while we talked quietly.
We agreed that honesty would always guide us, no matter what.

full_episode.title: Episode 20 — The Families Meet
text: Both families finally agreed to meet and plan the wedding together.
We met at a large restaurant with a beautiful garden view.
My mother brought sweets, and Layla's mother brought fresh coffee.
The fathers discussed the wedding budget and the guest list.
The mothers talked about the dress, the flowers, and the cake.
Layla's brother and Sami became close friends immediately.
Everyone laughed and shared old family stories with each other.
Children from both families played together near the garden fountain.
My grandmother told a long story about her own wedding day.
Everyone listened closely, smiling at the old, familiar details.
Layla's aunt brought a plate of homemade sweets for the table.
As the sun set, both families exchanged warm, sincere thanks.
Layla whispered to me: Our families really like each other.
I felt grateful watching everyone together, happy and united.
We chose the wedding date: the first Saturday of next month.
A photographer captured the two families laughing around one long table.
Watching everyone together, I felt truly lucky for the first time.

full_episode.title: Episode 21 — The Scheme
text: A few days before the wedding, Karim made a final, dangerous plan.
He called the wedding hall and tried to cancel our reservation.
The hall manager called me, confused about the cancellation request.
I explained that I never made that call. Someone was lying.
Sami investigated and discovered Karim's number behind the scheme.
I felt furious, but I remembered her father's advice: stay calm.
I confirmed our reservation again and added extra security for the day.
Karim also messaged a few relatives with false, confusing information.
One relative called my mother, worried about the strange rumors.
My mother stayed calm and simply asked for the real facts.
I called every relative personally to explain the situation clearly.
Slowly, the confusion faded, and trust returned to our families.
I told Layla the truth about Karim's dangerous scheme.
She was shocked and said: He needs to stop this immediately.
We decided to speak with Karim directly, one final time.
A quiet worry stayed with me even after the danger passed.
Layla held my hand and said calmly: We handled this together.

full_episode.title: Episode 22 — The Truth
text: Layla and I met Karim at a quiet cafe near the office.
Karim looked nervous and avoided looking directly at us.
Layla said firmly: We know about the wedding hall and the rumors.
Karim admitted everything. He said: I was jealous and afraid of losing you.
He apologized honestly and promised to stop causing trouble.
Layla said: I forgive you, but please respect our relationship now.
I felt a strange mix of anger and relief inside me.
Karim's hands shook slightly as he ordered a cup of tea.
He admitted he had been unhappy in his own job and life.
Layla spoke to him gently, without anger in her voice.
He thanked us quietly and said he would change his behavior.
As we left the cafe, I felt a strange sense of peace.
I said: Everyone deserves a second chance. I accept your apology.
Karim shook my hand and walked away, quiet and thoughtful.
Layla held my hand and said: Now, nothing can stop our wedding.
Walking away from that cafe, the air felt lighter somehow.
I hoped Karim would find his own peace, far from us.

full_episode.title: Episode 23 — Steady Hearts
text: Two days before the wedding, my family had one more important talk.
My father said: Marriage needs patience, respect, and honest communication.
My mother added: Always support Layla, even during difficult moments.
I promised my parents to protect and care for Layla forever.
Meanwhile, Layla faced her own doubts and nervous feelings.
Her mother said: It is normal to feel nervous before a big change.
Layla called me that night, just to hear my voice.
My father told an old family story about patience and faith.
I listened carefully, understanding why he wanted me to hear it.
Layla's mother gave her a small, old ring that belonged to her.
Layla said she would wear it proudly during the ceremony.
Both of us felt the weight and beauty of family history.
She said: I am ready for this new chapter of our life.
I said: Together, we can face anything that comes our way.
We stayed on the phone until we both fell asleep, calm and hopeful.
That night, stars filled the sky above our quiet neighborhood.
I fell asleep feeling grateful, calm, and completely ready for tomorrow.

full_episode.title: Episode 24 — The Night Before
text: The night before the wedding, our families gathered for a small dinner.
Layla's father stood up and gave a warm speech about love and family.
He said: I am proud to welcome you into our family, {{user_name}}.
My father replied with a speech about respect and lifelong partnership.
Layla and I exchanged small, meaningful gifts in front of everyone.
I gave her a bracelet, and she gave me a watch.
Sami raised his cup and said: To love, patience, and a happy future.
Sami helped arrange chairs and lights around the garden area.
Layla's sister practiced a short song for the ceremony.
My mother checked every small detail with careful, loving attention.
Neighbors stopped by to offer help and warm congratulations.
By midnight, everything felt ready for the most important day.
Everyone clapped, laughed, and celebrated the night before the big day.
Before we said goodnight, Layla whispered: Tomorrow, I become your wife.
I smiled and said: And I become the happiest man in {{user_city}}.
A soft breeze carried the smell of jasmine through the garden.
I looked at Layla and whispered: Tomorrow changes everything, beautifully.

full_episode.title: Episode 25 — The Wedding
text: The wedding day finally arrived, bright, warm, and full of joy.
Layla wore a beautiful white dress and held a bouquet of roses.
I stood at the hall, waiting nervously, wearing my best suit.
When Layla walked in, the whole room became quiet and warm.
Our parents watched with tears of joy in their eyes.
We said our vows in front of family, friends, and guests.
The judge said: You may now begin your life together.
Photographers captured every smile, every tear, every quiet glance.
Children threw flower petals as we walked out together.
Old friends and new neighbors joined the joyful celebration outside.
Under the evening sky, we danced our very first dance.
I looked at Layla and knew our real story was just starting.
Everyone celebrated with music, dancing, cake, and laughter.
Sami hugged me and said: You found real love. I am so proud.
As the night ended, Layla whispered: This is only the beginning of us.
The moon rose high above the garden as guests slowly left.
Hand in hand, Layla and I walked into our new life together.
